description: Update the docs site to reflect the current state of the codebase. Fix inaccurate CLI references, missing SDK utilities, wrong browser tool counts, incomplete config documentation, missing WebSocket protocol messages, and remove the plugin-specific Slack page. Maintain the existing friendly, hand-holding, step-by-step tone.
qualityChecks: cd docs && bun run build && bun run type-check && bun run lint && bun run knip

userStories.title: Fix CLI reference: add missing plugin subcommands (list, install, remove)
userStories.description: As a user reading the CLI docs, I want to see all available `opentabs plugin` subcommands so that I can manage plugins without guessing.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx documents `opentabs plugin list` with --json and --port flags
docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx documents `opentabs plugin install <name>` with shorthand expansion behavior
docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx documents `opentabs plugin remove <name>` including localPlugins cleanup
The text 'Has two subcommands: search and create' is replaced with accurate count (five subcommands)
cd docs && bun run build passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx. Currently at line 270 it says 'Manage plugins. Has two subcommands: search and create.' — change to five subcommands. Add three new subsections after the existing `opentabs plugin create` section (around line 350). Follow the existing table format using <Table> components. The `plugin install` command accepts shorthand names (e.g., `slack` expands to `opentabs-plugin-slack`). The `plugin list` command works in offline mode when server is not running. The `plugin remove` command also removes matching entries from the `localPlugins` config array.

userStories.title: Fix CLI reference: add missing config subcommands (reset, rotate-secret) and config set keys
userStories.description: As a user reading the CLI docs, I want to see all `opentabs config` subcommands so I know how to reset config or rotate my secret.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx documents `opentabs config reset` with --confirm flag
docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx documents `opentabs config rotate-secret`
The text 'Has three subcommands: show, set, and path' is replaced with accurate count (five subcommands)
The `config set` section documents all key formats: tool.<name>, browser-tool.<name>, port, localPlugins.add, localPlugins.remove
cd docs && bun run build passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx. Currently at line 362 it says 'View and manage configuration. Has three subcommands: show, set, and path.' — change to five. The existing `config set` section (line 395) only shows tool enable/disable. Add the other key formats. The `config reset` deletes the config file, requires `--confirm` flag. The `config rotate-secret` generates a new 256-bit random secret, writes to config.json and auth.json, notifies running server using old secret. Add new subsections after the existing `opentabs config path` section (around line 422).

acceptanceCriteria: docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx audit section includes --tool <name> flag

docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx audit section includes --file flag that reads from persistent disk log (~/.opentabs/audit.log)

userStories.notes: File: docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx. The audit table starts around line 132. Add three new rows to the existing <Table> component: `--tool <name>` (filter by tool name), `--since <duration>` (time filter, e.g. 30m, 1h, 2d), `--file` (read from persistent disk log at ~/.opentabs/audit.log instead of running server). userStories.title: Fix browser tools count and add plugin_analyze_site
userStories.description: As a user reading the browser tools docs, I want to see the correct tool count and the analyze_site tool so I know what's available.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/content/docs/reference/browser-tools.mdx includes plugin_analyze_site in a new 'Site Analysis' section
The total count in the summary table is 37 (not 36)
The title/description frontmatter says 37 instead of 36
docs/content/docs/index.mdx says 37 instead of 36
docs/content/docs/quick-start.mdx says 37 instead of 36
docs/content/docs/install/index.mdx says 37 instead of 36 (if present)
cd docs && bun run build passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/content/docs/reference/browser-tools.mdx. Add a new 'Site Analysis' section before the Summary table. `plugin_analyze_site` analyzes a web page to produce actionable intelligence for building plugins — it detects auth methods, APIs, frameworks, globals, DOM patterns, and storage keys. Input: { url: string, waitSeconds?: number }. It is classified in the 'interact' security tier. Update the summary table row counts: add Site Analysis (1, plugin_ prefix), change Total from 36 to 37. Also grep for '36 built-in' across ALL docs files and update to 37. Files to check: index.mdx, quick-start.mdx, install/index.mdx, reference/browser-tools.mdx.

userStories.title: Update SDK utilities page: add missing fetch, storage, and HTTP helper functions
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want to see all available SDK utility functions so I use the right helpers.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/content/docs/sdk/utilities.mdx documents putJSON(url, body, init?, schema?)
docs/content/docs/sdk/utilities.mdx documents patchJSON(url, body, init?, schema?)
docs/content/docs/sdk/utilities.mdx documents deleteJSON(url, init?, schema?)
docs/content/docs/sdk/utilities.mdx documents removeLocalStorage(key)
docs/content/docs/sdk/utilities.mdx documents setSessionStorage(key, value)
docs/content/docs/sdk/utilities.mdx documents removeSessionStorage(key)
docs/content/docs/sdk/utilities.mdx documents httpStatusToToolError(response, message)
docs/content/docs/sdk/utilities.mdx documents parseRetryAfterMs(value)
The import example at the top of the file includes the new functions
cd docs && bun run build passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/content/docs/sdk/utilities.mdx. Fetch section (starts around line 167): Add `putJSON`, `patchJSON`, `deleteJSON` after the existing `postJSON` section. `putJSON` and `patchJSON` have the same signature as `postJSON` (url, body, init?, schema?). `deleteJSON` is different — no body param, signature is (url, init?, schema?). All three support optional Zod schema validation as the last param. Also add `httpStatusToToolError(response, message)` which converts an HTTP Response to a ToolError, and `parseRetryAfterMs(value)` which parses a Retry-After header value to milliseconds. Storage section (starts around line 248): Add `removeLocalStorage(key)` after `setLocalStorage`, add `setSessionStorage(key, value)` after `getSessionStorage`, and `removeSessionStorage(key)` after `setSessionStorage`. Follow the existing doc style for each function — signature, brief description, code example.

userStories.title: Update configuration reference: add missing config fields and directory structure
userStories.description: As a user or plugin developer, I want the config reference to show all available fields so I can configure OpenTabs properly.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/content/docs/reference/configuration.mdx documents browserToolPolicy field
docs/content/docs/reference/configuration.mdx documents permissions object (trustedDomains, sensitiveDomains, toolPolicy, domainToolPolicy)
docs/content/docs/reference/configuration.mdx documents skipConfirmation field
docs/content/docs/reference/configuration.mdx documents skipSanitization field
The config directory structure section includes auth.json and audit.log
The config directory structure section shows extension/adapters/ subdirectory
The example config JSON is updated to show the new fields
cd docs && bun run build passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/content/docs/reference/configuration.mdx. The config fields table starts at line 17. Add new rows: `browserToolPolicy` (Record<string, boolean>, default {}), `permissions` (object with nested fields), `skipConfirmation` (boolean, optional), `skipSanitization` (boolean, optional). The permissions object contains: `trustedDomains` (string[], default ['localhost', '127.0.0.1']), `sensitiveDomains` (string[], default []), `toolPolicy` (Record<string, 'allow'|'ask'|'deny'>), `domainToolPolicy` (Record<string, Record<string, 'allow'|'ask'|'deny'>>). Also add the OPENTABS_SKIP_CONFIRMATION, OPENTABS_SKIP_SANITIZATION, and OPENTABS_SKIP_PLUGIN_VERIFICATION env vars to the env table. The config directory structure (line 175) needs auth.json, audit.log, and extension/adapters/ added. Keep the existing hand-holding tone — explain what each permission field does and when a user would change it.

userStories.notes: File: docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx. The /audit section (around line 169) has a query params table. Add `tool` (filter by tool name) and `success` (filter by true/false) rows. Change the `limit` default from 500 to 50 (the CLI default is 20, the HTTP default is 50, the max is 500). The /health section (around line 122) should note that unauthenticated responses return only { status, version, extensionConnected } while authenticated responses return the full payload. Add brief mention of disabledBrowserTools, confirmationBypassed, outputSanitizationEnabled fields to the 'Key fields' list.

acceptanceCriteria: docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx WebSocket server->extension table includes confirmation.request
docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx WebSocket extension->server table includes confirmation.response

userStories.notes: File: docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx (WebSocket Protocol section around line 287) and docs/content/docs/contributing/architecture.mdx (Wire Protocol section around line 230). Server -> Extension: add `confirmation.request` (Notification — human approval request for browser tools with id, tool, domain, tabId, paramsPreview, timeoutMs). Extension -> Server: add `confirmation.response` (Notification — approval decision with id, decision, scope) and `config.setAllToolsEnabled` (Request — toggle all tools for a plugin). Keep the same <Table> format.

userStories.title: Fix install page: remove incorrect --dev flag on opentabs start
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the install guide to give me correct commands.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/content/docs/install/index.mdx does NOT show `opentabs start --dev` as a valid command
The plugin developer section explains dev mode correctly (via bun --hot or OPENTABS_DEV=1 env var, not --dev on opentabs start)
cd docs && bun run build passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/content/docs/install/index.mdx. Around line 110, the 'For Plugin Developers' section says `opentabs start --dev`. The `opentabs start` command does NOT have a --dev flag — dev mode is only available when running the server directly with `bun --hot platform/mcp-server/dist/index.js --dev`. For plugin developers, they don't need dev mode at all since `opentabs-plugin build` calls POST /reload automatically. Remove the --dev flag reference and clarify that `opentabs start` handles reload via POST /reload when plugins are rebuilt. Keep the section simple and practical for plugin developers who just need a working setup.

No broken links to /docs/reference/slack-plugin exist in any docs .mdx file

CLAUDE.md says 'This file should remain plugin-agnostic. Do not enumerate individual plugins or tools by name.' The docs should follow the same principle. Delete the file and grep all .mdx files for any links to /docs/reference/slack-plugin — there may be references in other pages' 'Next Steps' sections. The page is already not in the sidebar nav (docs/config/navigation.ts), so no nav changes needed.

userStories.title: Add browser tool security tiers and confirmation system to architecture docs
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want the architecture page to document the permission evaluation engine and confirmation system.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/content/docs/contributing/architecture.mdx has a new section on browser tool security tiers (observe/interact/sensitive)
The section explains the permission evaluation order (domainToolPolicy > sensitiveDomains > toolPolicy > tier default > trustedDomains)
The section briefly describes the confirmation system (confirmation.request/response flow)
cd docs && bun run build passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/content/docs/contributing/architecture.mdx. Add a new subsection under the existing 'Security' section (starts around line 356). The three tiers are: observe (auto-allow — read-only tools like list_tabs, query_elements), interact (ask by default — actions like click, type, screenshot, navigate), sensitive (ask by default — execute_script, cookies, storage). The evaluation order is: domainToolPolicy[domain][tool] → sensitiveDomains match → toolPolicy[tool] → tier default → trustedDomains override (changes ask→allow, not deny→allow). Confirmation flow: server sends confirmation.request to extension → side panel shows approval UI → user decides (allow_once, allow_always with scope, or deny) → extension sends confirmation.response → server proceeds or rejects. Keep it concise — this is architecture reference, not a tutorial.

They follow the same pattern as postJSON.' Add one compact example showing putJSON. Keep it brief — the full API reference is on the Utilities page.
